SEC-HDR-HSTS-MISSING.name: Missing Strict-Transport-Security Header
SEC-HDR-HSTS-MISSING.description: The application does not enforce HSTS.
SEC-HDR-HSTS-MISSING.impact: Users are vulnerable to Man-in-the-Middle attacks.
SEC-HDR-HSTS-MISSING.remediation: Add the Strict-Transport-Security header to all responses.

SEC-HDR-HSTS-MISCONF.name: Misconfigured Strict-Transport-Security Header
SEC-HDR-HSTS-MISCONF.description: HSTS is disabled by setting max-age to 0 or has other misconfigurations.
SEC-HDR-HSTS-MISCONF.impact: Users are vulnerable to Man-in-the-Middle attacks.
SEC-HDR-HSTS-MISCONF.remediation: Set max-age to a suitable value (e.g., 31536000) and consider includeSubDomains.

SEC-HDR-XFO-MISSING.name: Missing X-Frame-Options Header
SEC-HDR-XFO-MISSING.description: The application does not use the X-Frame-Options header.
SEC-HDR-XFO-MISSING.impact: The application is vulnerable to Clickjacking (UI redressing) attacks.
SEC-HDR-XFO-MISSING.remediation: Implement the X-Frame-Options header with a value of DENY or SAMEORIGIN.

SEC-HDR-XFO-MISCONF.name: Misconfigured X-Frame-Options Header
SEC-HDR-XFO-MISCONF.description: X-Frame-Options is present but not set to DENY or SAMEORIGIN.
SEC-HDR-XFO-MISCONF.remediation: Set X-Frame-Options strictly to DENY or SAMEORIGIN.

SEC-HDR-XCTO-MISSING.name: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
SEC-HDR-XCTO-MISSING.description: The application is missing the X-Content-Type-Options header.
SEC-HDR-XCTO-MISSING.impact: Browsers may perform MIME-sniffing, potentially leading to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) if content is misinterpreted.
SEC-HDR-XCTO-MISSING.remediation: Add the X-Content-Type-Options header with the value 'nosniff' to all responses.

SEC-HDR-XCTO-MISCONF.name: Misconfigured X-Content-Type-Options Header
SEC-HDR-XCTO-MISCONF.description: X-Content-Type-Options is present but not set to 'nosniff'.
SEC-HDR-XCTO-MISCONF.impact: Browsers may execute files as unexpected types.
SEC-HDR-XCTO-MISCONF.remediation: Ensure X-Content-Type-Options is strictly set to 'nosniff'.

SEC-HDR-CSP-MISSING.name: Missing Content-Security-Policy Header
SEC-HDR-CSP-MISSING.description: The application does not define a Content Security Policy (CSP).
SEC-HDR-CSP-MISSING.impact: Increases the risk and potential impact of Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and other data injection attacks.
SEC-HDR-CSP-MISSING.remediation: Implement a robust Content-Security-Policy header to restrict resource loading to trusted sources.

INFO-DISC-SERVER-HEADER.name: Information Disclosure via Server Header
INFO-DISC-SERVER-HEADER.description: The web server exposes its identity and version through the 'Server' HTTP response header.
INFO-DISC-SERVER-HEADER.impact: Attackers can use the server version to search for known vulnerabilities and tailor their attacks against the specific technology stack.
INFO-DISC-SERVER-HEADER.remediation: Configure the web server to hide or obfuscate the 'Server' header (e.g., set 'ServerTokens Prod' or 'server_tokens off').

INFO-DISC-X-POWERED-BY.name: X-Powered-By Information Disclosure
INFO-DISC-X-POWERED-BY.description: The application exposes underlying framework or technology details via the 'X-Powered-By' HTTP response header.
INFO-DISC-X-POWERED-BY.impact: Revealing the backend technology assists attackers in identifying the application's architecture and potential framework-specific flaws.
INFO-DISC-X-POWERED-BY.remediation: Remove the 'X-Powered-By' header in the application framework or web server configuration.
